senses.definition: the products of making highly fashionable and expensive clothing

examples.x: The designer's latest haute couture collection dazzled the fashion world with its exquisite craftsmanship and innovative designs.
She wore a stunning haute couture gown to the gala, turning heads with its intricate embroidery and luxurious fabric.
Haute couture pieces are meticulously crafted by hand, often taking hundreds of hours to complete each garment.
The fashion show featured a parade of haute couture creations, each more elaborate and breathtaking than the last.
As an aspiring fashion designer, she dreams of one day presenting her own haute couture collection on the runways of Paris.

senses.definition: a type of shoe that is flat with a low heel, made of leather, and can be worn without fastening

examples.x: He slipped on a pair of comfortable loafers before heading out for a leisurely stroll around the neighborhood.
The classic penny loafer is a timeless style that pairs well with both casual and semi-formal outfits.
She wore suede loafers with tassels to the office, adding a touch of sophistication to her business attire.
The driver put on his driving loafers before getting behind the wheel, appreciating their flexibility and grip on the pedals.
The fashion-conscious man opted for a pair of brightly colored loafers to add a pop of personality to his ensemble.

senses.definition: a long, loose, flowing tunic or robe that is of Asian, African, or Middle Eastern origin

examples.x: She wore a flowing silk kaftan adorned with intricate embroidery for her beachside wedding ceremony.
The kaftan is a versatile garment, perfect for lounging at home or dressing up for a casual outing.
He slipped into a lightweight linen kaftan to stay cool in the hot desert climate.

senses.definition: a richly decorative fabric characterized by raised patterns

examples.x: The curtains in the room were made of luxurious brocade fabric.
He admired the brocade vest displayed in the shop window.
The queen's throne was upholstered in regal brocade.
The royal tapestries were woven with exquisite brocade detailing.
The designer used brocade fabric to create a glamorous evening coat.

senses.definition: items of clothing and accessories for men

examples.x: The haberdashery displayed an array of men's accessories, including ties, pocket squares, and cufflinks, adding a touch of sophistication to any ensemble.

senses.definition: a textured fabric with intricate woven patterns, commonly found in high-end clothing

examples.x: His tailored suit had subtle jacquard detailing on the lapels.
The designer handbag was crafted from luxurious jacquard fabric.
Her winter coat was lined with jacquard silk for added warmth and style.

senses.definition: flowing gown worn by women in the late 17th and early 18th centuries

examples.x: The mantua featured elaborate embroidery along the bodice.
The mantua's bodice was cinched tightly with a satin ribbon.
She draped her mantua gracefully over her arm as she entered the ballroom.
The mantua's rich fabric shimmered in the candlelight of the evening soiree.
A mantua was considered fashionable attire for formal occasions in the 18th century.

senses.definition: a decorative frill or extension of fabric that is attached at the waist of a garment

examples.x: The dress featured a flattering peplum that accentuated her waist and added a touch of flair to the silhouette.
She opted for a blouse with a peplum detail, creating a stylish and feminine look for the office.
The designer added a dramatic peplum to the hem of the skirt, giving it a modern and sophisticated twist.
The bridesmaid dresses were designed with a delicate lace peplum, adding elegance and romance to the ensemble.
The fashion trend of the season featured tops and jackets with exaggerated peplums, making a bold statement on the runway.

senses.definition: a part of a garment that is constructed from two or more layers of fabric, typically located at the shoulder or waist

examples.x: The shirt had a decorative yoke embellished with intricate embroidery, adding visual interest to the neckline.
She admired the tailored jacket with a contrasting yoke, which gave it a modern and stylish look.
The dress featured a fitted bodice with a yoke detail that accentuated her figure and provided extra support.
The denim jacket had a Western-style yoke across the back, giving it a rugged and authentic appearance.
The traditional blouse had a gathered yoke at the neckline, creating a relaxed and comfortable fit.

senses.definition: a teardrop-shaped, intricate design pattern characterized by a curved, abstract floral motif

examples.x: She loved the intricate swirls and teardrop shapes of paisley, finding them endlessly fascinating.
The paisley in the fabric's design added a touch of sophistication and charm to the dress.
The fashion designer incorporated paisley into her latest collection, drawing inspiration from vintage patterns.
He admired the craftsmanship of the handmade rug, particularly the paisley woven into its intricate design.

senses.definition: a long, loose-fitting dressing gown, usually worn by women after taking a bath or shower

examples.x: She lounged in her luxurious silk peignoir, feeling elegant and glamorous.
The bride-to-be wore a delicate lace peignoir while getting ready for her wedding day.
The actress draped a chiffon peignoir over her shoulders as she prepared for her close-up on set
The peignoir set included a matching robe and nightgown, perfect for relaxing evenings at home.
She wrapped herself in her favorite peignoir before settling down with a book and a cup of tea.

senses.definition: a medieval garment worn primarily by women, consisting of a cloth covering the head, neck, and sometimes the chin, often worn beneath a veil or hood

examples.x: The medieval nun wore a white wimple as part of her habit.
Her wimple framed her face elegantly, accentuating her features.
The wimple was secured under her chin with a simple clasp.
A wimple was a common head covering for women in the Middle Ages.
The nun's wimple was made of soft linen fabric for comfort.

senses.definition: a crisp, smooth fabric with a slight sheen, commonly used in fashion for formal garments

examples.x: Her bridesmaid dress was made of shimmering pink taffeta.
The tailor used taffeta lining to add structure to the jacket.
She felt like a princess in her floor-length taffeta gown.
The flower girl's dress had a sash made of blue taffeta.
The prom dress was crafted from iridescent purple taffeta.

senses.definition: clothing or garments, especially when considered in terms of fashion or formal attire

examples.x: She selected a beautiful gown from her raiment for the gala event.
The king's raiment was adorned with intricate embroidery and jewels.
In her wardrobe, there was a vast array of raiment, ranging from casual to formal attire.
He carefully folded his raiment and placed it in the suitcase for his trip.
The boutique offered a selection of stylish raiment for customers to choose from.

senses.definition: skilled dressmaker or fashion designer, particularly in the context of historical fashion

examples.x: The modiste crafted a custom gown for the client's special occasion.
She visited the modiste's shop to have her wedding dress tailored.
Design sketches adorned the walls of the modiste's studio.
The modiste's assistant meticulously pinned fabric for alterations.
Fashion students admired the modiste's vintage sewing techniques during the workshop.

senses.definition: an additional item or clothing for a specific activity

examples.x: The soldier's uniform was complete with all necessary accouterments, including a helmet, rifle, and canteen.
She meticulously organized her kitchen drawers, ensuring that every utensil and cooking accouterment had its designated place.
The chef's apron was adorned with various culinary accouterments, such as measuring spoons and a thermometer.
The explorer packed his backpack with essential accouterments for the journey, including a compass, map, and first aid kit.
The magician's act was accompanied by various accouterments, including decks of cards, silk scarves, and a top hat.

senses.definition: a small, ornamental handbag or purse, typically carried by women in the 18th and 19th centuries as a fashion accessory

examples.x: She carried a delicate reticule to the ball, matching her gown.
The fashionable lady's reticule was adorned with intricate beadwork.
The reticule's silk fabric shimmered in the candlelight of the ballroom.
The reticule hung gracefully from her wrist as she walked.
Inside the reticule, she kept a small mirror and a vial of perfume.

senses.definition: a fancy decoration added to clothes for style

examples.x: The dress was adorned with a furbelow of lace along the hem.
Her blouse featured a furbelow of ruffles cascading down the front.
She added a furbelow of ribbon to her skirt for a playful touch.
The jacket had a furbelow of beads sewn onto the collar.
Her gloves had a furbelow of lace at the cuffs.

senses.definition: the art, craft, or business of designing, making, or selling hats, especially women's

examples.x: After attending a millinery workshop, Jenna discovered her passion for designing intricate and elegant women's hats.
She admired the exquisite millinery displayed in the boutique window.
The millinery shop on Elm Street has been a family business for generations, known for its exquisite and unique hat designs.
Enrolled in a course on millinery, Sarah learned various techniques for making hats, from blocking to embellishing with feathers and lace.
The city's most famous millinery boasts an impressive collection of handcrafted hats, attracting fashion enthusiasts from around the world.

senses.definition: a delicate metalwork, like jewelry, with intricate designs made from thin wires or threads
a decorative plume or feather worn as a fashion accessory on hats or helmets

examples.x: The knight adorned his helmet with a vibrant panache.
The king's courtiers sported hats embellished with colorful panaches.
His Renaissance doublet featured a panache of ostrich plumes.
She wore her Tudor gown with a panache of feathers.
The queen's courtiers wore panaches as a symbol of their allegiance.

senses.definition: a stiff, petticoat-like undergarment made of horsehair or a similar material, which is worn to give shape and volume to a woman's skirt

examples.x: In the 19th century, women often wore crinolines under their dresses to achieve a voluminous silhouette.
The ball gown was supported by layers of crinoline, giving it a full and dramatic skirt.
The costume designer used crinoline to give the ballet dancers' tutus their characteristic shape and structure.
The fashion designer incorporated crinoline into the wedding dress, adding layers of tulle for a romantic and ethereal effect.

senses.definition: a long, elegant coat worn often in the 19th century

examples.x: His pelisse was tailored to fit snugly over his military uniform.
His pelisse hung elegantly as he strode through the ballroom.
With his pelisse on, he looked ready for the cold weather.
Despite the rain, he kept dry under his pelisse.
People admired the prince's fancy pelisse.

senses.definition: characterized by custom-made clothing tailored to an individual's preferences
referring to clothing, particularly men's clothing, or the manner in which it is tailored or worn

examples.x: His sartorial choices reflected his impeccable taste and attention to detail, always opting for tailored suits and polished shoes.
The fashion designer's sartorial creations were celebrated for their innovative designs and luxurious fabrics.
She admired the sartorial elegance of the men at the event, impressed by their well-fitted suits and stylish accessories.
The magazine featured a spread on sartorial trends for the season, showcasing the latest in haute couture and street style.
The tailor was known for his mastery of sartorial craftsmanship, producing garments that were both stylish and impeccably constructed.

senses.definition: stitched together in a decorative pattern, creating a padded or textured surface

examples.x: She wore a quilted jacket to stay warm in the chilly weather.
The quilted handbag added a touch of elegance to her outfit.
She admired the quilted pattern on her friend's tote bag.
She wrapped herself in a quilted blanket while reading a book by the fireplace.

senses.definition: (of a fabric or piece of clothing) made with many small folds or pleats

examples.x: She chose a ruched blouse for its figure-flattering design, accentuating her curves in all the right places.
The ruched sleeves of the jacket added a feminine twist to the otherwise classic silhouette.
The bride's gown featured a ruched waistline, creating a romantic and ethereal look for her wedding day.

senses.definition: (typically of a man) stylish and neat in appearance, often characterized by well-groomed attire and attention to detail
required by etiquette or fashion norms, indicating it's currently popular or expected to wear

examples.x: Wearing a black tie to the event is de rigueur.
For formal occasions, a tailored suit is de rigueur for gentlemen.
At the gala, wearing heels was de rigueur for the ladies.
In the workplace, business attire is de rigueur for professional appearances.
Sunglasses are de rigueur for protecting your eyes and adding flair to your outfit.

senses.definition: high collar, often stiff, that encircles the neck and may extend to cover the shoulders, typically found in formal or historical attire

examples.x: She wore a gown with an intricate lace gorget.
She adorned her evening gown with a sparkling gorget necklace.
His Renaissance costume included a velvet gorget.
The Victorian dress had a delicate lace gorget collar.
She wrapped a colorful gorget around her head as a stylish headband.
